label: Wide-shell 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, GR style — front band: great_room (left) + standard bedroom (right). Rear band: master + ensuite (left), hall + common T&B stacked (hall in front, T&B behind it) in the middle, kitchen (right). Hall is the circulation hinge: open to kitchen, and to great_room behind it, and hosts master's and common's only doors. Neither a clean vertical split nor a horizontal split — private and public occupy diagonal corners instead.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — carved from master's own footprint via the alcove claim. Door from master only, no other access.
ensuite and common bath (T&B) share a plumbing wall, consolidating both wet stacks in the rear-middle band. No door — ensuite stays master-exclusive per the locked catalog convention.
master's east wall shares a wall with hall — direct door, master's only route to the rest of the house.

master's front wall = great_room's rear wall — geometry-only, NO door. Master's actual public access is via hall, not directly into great_room. Same idiom as master↔great in 1s_2br_wd_l_wrap_bath_hall_gr.
T&B's door opens into the hall — matches the catalog's standard 'common bath is hall/public-accessible, not routed through a bedroom' rule.
hall's front wall is an open mouth into great_room — codebase rule: hallway↔public is always open-plan, no door. This is hall's (and by extension master's and common's) connection to the public wing.
kitchen's west wall opens onto the hall (open-plan, no door) — hall serves as kitchen's entrance from the private/circulation side, per brief.

standard's rear wall = kitchen's front wall — geometry-only, NO door. Keeps the building outline a clean rectangle at this seam, but deliberately does not give a bedroom a direct opening onto the kitchen work area; standard's actual door is to great_room instead.
standard's only door — direct into great_room. 1.1 m floor = 0.8 m clear panel + frame + a little leeway.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_quadrant_split_baths_ds_ld
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: The source concept sketch draws ensuite very small (~1.5 x 1.5 m, ~2.25 sqm) — tighter than every other ensuite in the catalog (which float around 2.5-3.0 sqm). auto_apply floors it at 2.2 sqm to stay close to the drawn concept while keeping it just above the 1.2 sqm hard minimum used elsewhere; preferred_apply lets it grow to 2.5 sqm where the lot allows. Kitchen and common bath floors match the rest of the wide-shell catalog.

setback_elements.note: kitchen sits in the rear band in this topology, so the standard rear_setback dirty-kitchen pattern applies normally.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED, QUADRANT split, GR style. Revised 2026-07-14: living + dining merged into a single great_room (was two separate rooms), which eliminates a 3-way mutual adjacency (hall/kitchen/dining all touching each other) that made the original living+dining version unsolvable even on a 34x34 m lot with every room's size caps relaxed. Also fixes an authoring bug in the original: hall was declared both rear_anchored AND had common stacked behind it via front_to_rear_stacks — the same 'forced to touch the rear wall while another room sits behind it' contradiction diagnosed (and fixed) twice already this project on other topologies. hall is no longer rear_anchored here; common (T&B) is, correctly, since it's the one that actually touches the rear wall.
Layout intent (canonical form, street/front at the bottom):
Rear band  (top):    [master + ensuite | hall / common T&B (stacked) | kitchen ]
great_room spans the full left+middle width (stacked in front of both master and the hall/common column); standard spans just the right width, stacked in front of kitchen only.

Circulation: hall is the hinge between public and private. great_room -> hall (open mouth, no door) -> master (door) and common T&B (door). hall also opens directly onto kitchen (open-plan) — kitchen's only route to/from the rest of the house besides being visually open to it. kitchen has no direct adjacency to great_room; you reach it via hall.
standard's only door is to great_room. standard<->kitchen is geometry-only (wall, no door) — deliberately no direct bedroom-to-kitchen opening.

zone_split is null here (unlike the vertical- and horizontal-split topologies) — the private/public boundary isn't a single axis, so per-room 'zone' tags carry the classification instead. Same convention already used by 1s_2br_wd_l_wrap_bath_hall_gr.
Confirmed target_shell: wide — the sketch's ~10x7 m footprint (1.43:1) matches the wide-shell catalog's proportions much more closely than squarish.
Floor band: roughly 65-85 sqm buildable, sized against the sketch's ~70 sqm footprint (10 x 7 m building on a 14x11 buildable envelope).
No fallback declared yet — this is a first-draft concept, not validated against a compact-lot fallback the way the rest of the catalog is.
